attacking_style: Relies primarily on sustained climbing pace rather than explosive attacks. Shows ability to follow key selections and maintain position in elite GC groups through consistent power output.
team_tactics: Bahrain employs elimination tactics through brutal sustained tempo-setting (multiple observations). Can burn through domestiques quickly with aggressive pacing strategies, sometimes misjudging resource management.
terrain_preference: Excels on high-altitude sustained climbs (2135m+ demonstrated strength). Performs well in prolonged grinding efforts that favor steady power over explosive accelerations. Shows competence in time trials under pressure.
vulnerability: Poor pacing judgment when domestiques commit early—suffers when GC attacks come after team resources exhausted. Struggles when left without team support, though shows individual resilience to limit losses.
key_patterns: Sets or benefits from brutal elimination pace on climbs (3 instances across Vuelta)
Makes key GC selections, distancing top riders like Bernal/Yates through sustained efforts
Demonstrates high-altitude climbing strength above 2000m (Catalunya, Vuelta mountain stages)
Resource management issues: burns domestiques early then vulnerable to later attacks (2 documented instances)
Strong mental resilience in ITTs and when isolated, holding positions despite pressure
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 observation shows continued climbing strength in support role, but limited recent GC leadership data to assess current podium-contending form versus 2021 Vuelta
